title: Driver-stint local ranking model for 'pit now vs wait'
summary: Learning-to-rank objectives are built for choosing the best item inside a query group.
rationale: Learning-to-rank objectives are built for choosing the best item inside a query group. Here, the natural query is not the race lap across drivers but the driver-race-stint sequence across candidate laps, where the true stop lap should outrank nearby non-stop laps. That is a different supervision shape than the current global classifier and should produce a useful complementary model.
implementation_hint: For each driver-race-stint, build query groups over laps before the actual pit. Label the realized stop lap highest, optionally give near-stop laps graded relevance by distance-to-stop, and train XGBRanker or CatBoost ranking with qid/group_id on the stint. Convert rank scores to next-lap probabilities via OOF sigmoid calibration and blend with the classifier.
expected_effect: Can sharpen ordering inside ambiguous windows where many consecutive laps look similar under plain binary loss.
risk: If queries are too long or mostly trivial, the ranker may learn stint length more than stop timing; calibration back to probabilities must be strictly OOF.
